notes: Game works native and on Proton out of the box. I installed manually D9VK and it worked as well with this DX9 to Vulkan translation, but there are some graphical glitches.
title: Psychonauts

notes: Game works native on Linux. On Proton it works out of the box. I installed manually D9VK and it worked as well fine with this DX9 to Vulkan translation.
title: Psychonauts

notes: Fresh install of KDE Neon with nothing else but Steam installed. Keyboard binding issues appear right out of the gate when forcing Proton. (Native version is OpenGL 2.0, incompatible with FreeSync) Wined3d for DX9 has performance hiccups, but does support FreeSync... D9VK requires protontricks (and thus winetricks) to get the d3d9x and d3dcompiler_43 packages for the shaders to work, and PS3 controllers, even though Xinput translation happens with other games and Proton, is completely absent. All this to get FreeSync working.
title: Psychonauts

notes: Update: Whoops, I was using the D-Pad to navigate the menus, when that only works with the analog sticks. Bindings are messed up to the point they have to be reset each time the game loads a new level. Framerate issues with wined3d were resolved when I took out a bad environment variable in my install "__GL_MaxFramesAllowed=1". Binding issues make the Proton version frustrating.
title: Psychonauts

notes: The native linux version seems to have major issues with controls and would  turn off my other two monitors. Forcing proton in the steam game settings seems to have fixed the other two monitors shutting off and somewhat fixed the controls. I still had issues at first trying to play with a xbox elite controller, but I went into the menus and hit default controls and that seemed to fixed my control issues. I did notice that the game gets a little buggy when climbing or swinging from poles. The jump button doesn't seem to register sometimes. I don't remember that ever being an issue on windows, but overall the game has run really well.
title: Psychonauts

notes: Even though Psychonauts runs natively on Linux, getting the inputs to work on either my keyboard or ps4 controller was proving impossible. After forcing Proton to run, the game itself runs with no notable differences from the native version with the benefit that both my keyboard and ps4 controller work properly now. If you have no problem with your inputs I would suggest running it natively, but if you do, you could try forcing Proton on, it runs just as well as the native version for me.
title: Psychonauts

notes: D9VK no longer has graphical issues, but the Wine 1000hz mice polling issue is VERY apparent in this game. Make sure to use a standard mouse with a lower polling rate or frame times will suffer as a result. Had trouble with bindings on a Dualshock 3 controller.
title: Psychonauts

notes: In both the native version and through forcing Proton, there's an issue with the bindings in that they become messed up when you boot the game up and load a profile or saved game. Just reset the bindings in the options for controls to work properly. Kb+m and an Xbox 360 controller were used here.
title: Psychonauts
